title: cassia indochinensis
intro: Cassia indochinensis, also known as Senna auriculata, is a plant species belonging to the Legume family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Cassia indochinensis include its use in treating digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its ability to lower blood sugar levels and alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. Cassia indochinensis can be prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, with some of its common herbal preparations including decoctions, infusions, and tinctures.
